exact_quote: Yes approved. You only need to ask for permission on next phase big decisions
quote_context: Direct chat reply to the proposal of a narrow standing relaunch approval for transport-only capability-preflight relaunches, following the r2 timeout halt. The reply both approves that proposal and broadens the standing scope to operational decisions within the current phase.

granted: capability-preflight relaunches whose diff from the last owner-named execution identity touches only transport settings (timeouts, streaming pins, SDK retry pinning), verified by the allowed-diff validator, with models, prompts, seeds, token limits, caps, and scope byte-unchanged

operational decisions within the capability-preflight stage and its analysis under the existing approved caps, including reconciliation-resolution artifacts, anchor selection by the frozen mechanical rule, archival, and reporting
each relaunch remains fully manifested, validated fail-closed, and recorded append-only

still_requires_explicit_owner_approval: any cap increase or scope change
any diff to models, prompts, seeds, token limits, or scientific content
next-phase decisions generally, per the owner's wording
note: Standing owner delegation for transport-tuning relaunches and in-phase operational decisions. Grants no execution authority by itself; every relaunch still flows through an execution manifest and authorization record binding this artifact as its approval basis.
